architecture.summary: TEMPO trains LLMs to enforce temporal discipline during backtesting by learning to condition evidence selection on instance-specific cutoff dates. A two-mode reward function (leakage mode + performance mode) is applied within a GRPO-based training pipeline. The leakage mode drives post-cutoff claims toward zero as a prerequisite; once temporal compliance is achieved, the performance mode optimizes task accuracy. The model learns to discover temporally valid reasoning strategies without erasing knowledge.

biblio.title: TEMPO: Temporal Enforcement via Mode-Separated Policy Optimization for Trustworthy LLM Backtesting

claims.statement: TEMPO reduces leakage from 2~13% to 0.6~3.7% across all conditions
task performance improving 6~13% where strong pre-cutoff signals exist

claims.dataset: prediction tasks without strong pre-cutoff signals
claims.statement: task performance maintained where the prediction task is inherently difficult from valid information alone
training monotonically decreases leakage, converges to the leak-free optimum, and improves task performance once compliance is achieved
Prompt-based constraints fail when suppressed content is causally related to the prediction
knowledge unlearning cannot address temporal compliance because it is instance-specific: the same fact may be legitimate evidence for one cutoff date and a violation for another
